Iberia Líneas Aéreas España, S.A. (Iberia Airlines Spain English), usually shortened Iberia, largest airline Spain, based Madrid.
years following World large advancement aircraft technology with concomitant increase possibilities rapid connections between international business concerns. Germany many business concerns, particularly Africa South America, still reliant ships land routes reach these. order circumvent Treaty Versailles stipulation that prohibited German aircraft access flying over France, agents Spain explored possibility liaison between Germany Spain. such Deutsche Luft Hansa behalf German government, established general agreement with Spanish government which signed December 1927 authorizing service between both countries.
Iberia, Compañía Aérea Transportes incorporated June 1927 with initial capital investment financier Horacio Echeberrieta Deutsche Luft Hansa million pesetas. company obtained Government authorisation establish commercial daily (except Sunday) connections between Madrid Barcelona. fleet three Rohrbach Roland monoplanes were bought from Deutsche Luft Hansa.
Flight operations started December 1927 with inaugural flights aimed coincide commemorative show Madrid. opening ceremony Carabanchel aerodrome attended Alfonso XIII president Iberia, Horacio Echeberrieta. maiden flight from Madrid left schedule, taking from Madrid 12:30 arriving Barcelona hours later. However Barcelona Madrid flight briefly forced stop Almazán, Soria reduced visibility caused snow. Although this flight 9:00, aiming arrive Madrid midday, delay meant arrived Madrid hours after dignitaries already left.
Passengers front Iberia Breguet Limousine 1931.
Within year, company sponsored government provide postal transport between Madrid Barcelona. Meanwhile, Deutsche Luft Hansa favourably rewarded investment Iberia company when granted regular service between Germany Spain. January 1928 route Barcelona Marseilles Geneva Zürich Stuttgart Leipzig/Halle Erfurt Berlin initiated. July 1928, twin-engined Dornier added fleet. This seaplane used pioneering route between Cádiz Canary Islands. This used test viability postal route South America, extending route Cape Verde Islands.
During dictatorship Miguel Primo Rivera, proposed that aviation companies Spain should combined become state-controlled general interest public utility. This came into effect early 1928. consequence, Iberia merged into Compañía Líneas Aéreas Subvencionadas S.A. (C.L.A.S.S.A.) together with following other airlines Unión Aérea Española (U.A.E.), Compañía Española Tráfico Aéreo (C.E.T.A.) pilot training company Compañía Española Aviación (C.E.A.) airship line Transaérea Colón.
Although Iberia ceased activities 1929, barely eighteen months after having begun operations, name "Iberia" continued registered Director-General Daniel Araoz Aréjula. airlines that formed CLASSA, Iberia would only keep existence after merger.
Following proclamation Second Spanish Republic 1931 LAPE (Líneas Aéreas Postales Españolas) company, airline that would replace CLASSA, became established. LAPE began operations April 1932, CLASSA flying under Spanish Republican Flag year.
LAPE operated even during Spanish Civil War, mainly Madrid Barcelona line, although great part planes fleet were requisitioned Spanish Republican Force used military transports.
name "Iberia" still registered, used anew when airline operations began 1937 territory held rebel faction during Spanish Civil War. 1939, after conflict, planes belonging LAPE's fleet were repainted with Iberia airline livery.
When Iberia company fully restarted operations following Civil War, became purely domestic airline. right many international routes, such service between Madrid Lisbon; which been previously established early 1927 Unión Aérea Española (U.A.E.).
Inaugural flight Bata (Ecuatorial Guinea) 1941.
eighth Iberia's capital investment offered Littoria, fascist Italy's airline. After some deliberation, negative experience investing related Corporacion Americana Servicios Aéreos, Littoria decided invest capital Iberia form aircraft which were join Iberia's fleet. Iberia's shares were held Spanish government (51%) private investors (49%). Foreign interests could only maximum Iberia shares allocated foreign investment. Littoria purchased three airframes (without engines) from Lufthansa gave them Iberia lieu capital. aircraft were work initially route.
company still close ties with Lufthansa, which during World caused considerable difficulties maintain fleet import fuel spare parts infrequent. addition Allied Powers refused cooperation with company until connections with Nazi Germany were severed.

airline nationalised September 1944, becoming part INI. 1946, using Douglas DC-4 Madrid Montevideo Buenos Aires route, became first airline between Europe South America after WWII. lucrative market South America helped families separated Civil War. However, there intense competition from France, British South American Airways. Since company struggling meet domestic demand, Government allowed another company, Aviaco, operate domestic routes that Iberia from February 1948.
Pact Madrid 1953, visa requirements were eliminated visitors Spain. This stimulated commencement transatlantic flights between Spain United States following year. addition, amendments made Montreal Convention International Civil Aviation June 1954 were liberal Spain, allowing impetus mass tourism using charter planes. Iberia responded increased traffic acquiring fleet Lockheed Super Constellation long range aircraft these served routes North South America from 1954. pressurised twin-engined Convair introduced company's European routes from 1957.
From 1961 Iberia fleet Douglas DC-8 four-engined long range airliners. busiest route Madrid Buenos Aires. 1965 joint board Iberia Aviaco coordinate policies that services conflict. early 1970s company bought Douglas DC-9s Boeing 747s expanded routes Central America, Warsaw, Athens Istanbul.
1974 launched Europe's first walk-on shuttle service, linking Madrid Barcelona. That year established Serviberia telephone information service, precursor today's popular call centres, well 'Red Jackets', uniformed ground staff with mission providing special service customers resolve problems that might arise.
Boeing 747-400 2006.
50th anniversary 1977 airline carried over million passengers year first time. There fierce internal political debates over Douglas DC-9s Boeing 727s; with result that Iberia bought Boeing 727s thereby boosting Boeing's flagging production line. However this financial commitment subsequent failure anticipate predicted passenger numbers, caused heavy losses company during 1980s. This especially disappointing 1982, with visit Pope football World Cup. 1987 routes Aviv, Khartoum, Moscow Tokyo were added.
late 1980s/early 1990s Iberia planned fleet renewal with McDonnell Douglas MD-87, Airbus A320 Airbus A340 replacing Douglas DC-9, Boeing Douglas DC-10 respectively. number Boeing 757s were also bought.
this time Iberia began build interests other Spanish airlines Aviaco, Viva Air, Binter Canarias Binter Mediterráneo Latin American airlines Aerolíneas Argentinas, Viasa Ladeco. June 1990 company consortium Aerolíneas Argentinas agreed $2billion stake following year bought stake Viasa $145million.
1991 Iberia Europe's first international airline frequent-flyer programme, Iberia Plus, and, 1996, airline launched www.iberia.com website. company ordered aircraft from Airbus February 1998, which largest single consignment Airbus ordered, bought Aviaco 1999 inherited fleet. This start Iberia's shift from Boeing/McDonnell Douglas fleet Airbus fleet.
this stage privatisation calls from European Union Spanish Government were being implemented. December 1999 company became semi-privatised with shares distributed shareholders, British Airways €245million American Airlines. initial public offering October 2000 failed ahead, internal business difficulties, most notably failure offload stock Aerolíneas Argentinas.
Iberia international transportation group, operating about airports countries, employing more than 26,000 people.
2002, Iberia celebrated 75th anniversary making oldest airlines world. During those years nearly million people have been flown Iberia, placing company among five largest European airlines, important routes connecting Spain with Europe Latin America.
Iberia Group encompasses Iberia Regional/Air Nostrum franchise. addition transporting passengers freight, Iberia carries many related activities, such aircraft maintenance, handling airports, systems, in-flight catering, holiday packages.
Iberia Group airlines destinations countries. code-sharing arrangements with other companies, offers flights another destinations countries. With fleet almost aircraft, makes about 1,000 flights each day. 2002, Iberia carried million passengers 210,000 tons freight.
addition, Iberia aircraft maintenance company, servicing fleet those another companies, including some European airlines.[which?] Iberia main supplier aircraft handling services Spanish airports, with more than airline clients.
Iberia founding partner computerised ticket reservation system Amadeus CRS, with 18.28% stake this sold 2005. also tour operator through Viva Tours Tiempo Libre units, with Cacesa supplies package delivery services.
Iberia allied with American Airlines British Airways, September 1999, joined Oneworld alliance. British Airways owns share capital.
July 2004, Iberia announced decided move Latin American from Miami, Florida Pedro Sula, Honduras. Although rumoured plans that airline considering Luis Muñoz Marín International Airport were discarded with announcement, Iberia announced that plans expand Puerto Rican schedules connect Juan with Central American cities.
2005, Iberia introduced Business Plus Class, Airbus A340 aircraft.
February 2006 Terminal Madrid Barajas awarded Iberia Oneworld alliance members. This provided expansion capabilities Iberia. Iberia alone responsible around Madrid Barajas traffic. 2005 Iberia regional branch Nostrum transported 21,619,041 passengers to/from Madrid Barajas alone.
November 2009, Iberia Airlines confirmed that reached preliminary agreement merge with British Airways. merger between carriers will create world's third-largest airline terms revenue. April 2010, confirmed that British Airways Iberia agreed merger, forming International Airlines Group, although each airline would continue operate under current brand. International Airlines Group, established head office London registered office Madrid, operating mainly from main hubs London Heathrow Airport Madrid Barajas Airport with secondary hubs London Gatwick Airport Barcelona Prat Airport.
December 1948 Douglas DC-3 crashed weather near Gandesa killing occupants.
March 1973, people were killed mid-air collision above French city Nantes involving Iberia Douglas DC-9 flying from Palma London Flight 504; Convair Coronado aircraft, operating Spantax Flight 400. Spantax Convair able make successful emergency landing whilst Iberia DC-9 crashed killing everyone board. accident occurred during French traffic controllers' strike.
December 1973, Iberia McDonnell Douglas DC-10 registered EC-CBN overran runway upon landing Boston Logan after flight from Madrid Airport. There were fatalities amongst people board, however aircraft written off.
February 1985, Iberia Flight 610, Boeing 727-256 crashed after hitting television antenna installed summit Monte while landing Bilbao, killing people.
July 1996 Iberia Flight 6621, McDonnell-Douglas DC-10-30 flying from Madrid Havana hijacked mid-flight. hijacker, Lebanese national named Ibrahim Saada demanded flight diverted Miami International Airport. Saada later apprehended faced years prison. hurt flight later landed Jose Marti International Airport some hours later.
November 2007 Iberia Airbus A340-600, registration EC-JOH, badly damaged after sliding runway Mariscal Sucre International Airport. injuries were reported. According Airbus, aircraft written-off.
